title: Nested podman is not working
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

Running podman inside of podman doesn't work.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Run `podman run --rm -ti quay.io/podman/stable podman run --rm -ti hello-world`

**Describe the results you received:**

~~~
$ podman run --rm -ti quay.io/podman/stable podman run --rm -ti hello-world
Error: mount /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay, flags: 0x1000: operation not permitted
~~~

**Describe the results you expected:**

Output when trying the same with docker:

~~~
$ docker run --rm -ti  -v /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock  docker run --rm -ti hello-world

Hello from Docker!
This message shows that your installation appears to be working correctly.
…
~~~

*Why is this needed?* In order to do a containerized build and then run integration tests from this environment, including additional containers like postgres, which are orchestrated by the systemtests (run inside a container).

Currently this excludes people running podman from using our integration tests.

Also, this seems to work inside of GitHub actions, when using podman or docker. However, I am not sure what their environment looks like in detail.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.2.1
API Version:  2.1.0
Go Version:   go1.15.5
Built:        Tue Dec  8 15:37:50 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body:
As a workaround, I just tried to run docker inside of podman (which would solve the issue for us) using "podman remote". That would mean a bit of extra work for podman users, but still work. However, that fails as well:

~~~
$ podman run --rm -ti  -v $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/podman/podman.sock:/var/run/docker.sock:z docker.io/library/docker run --rm -ti hello-world
docker: Got permission denied while trying to connect to the Docker daemon socket at unix:///var/run/docker.sock: Post http://%2Fvar%2Frun%2Fdocker.sock/v1.24/containers/create: dial unix /var/run/docker.sock: connect: permission denied.
See 'docker run --help'.
~~~

The socket seems available:

~~~
$ ls $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/podman/ -la
total 0
drwxr-xr-x.  2 me me  60 Jan 29 10:53 ./
drwx------. 19 me me 620 Jan 29 10:52 ../
srw-------.  1 me me   0 Jan 29 10:53 podman.sock=
~~~

And `podman-remote info` spits out all kind of information.

The socket also seems available inside the initial container:

~~~
$ podman run --rm -ti  -v $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/podman/podman.sock:/var/run/docker.sock:z docker.io/library/docker ls /var/run/docker.sock -la
srw-------    1 root     root             0 Jan 29 09:55 /var/run/docker.sock
~~~

This works for me:
```
$ podman run --rm -ti --privileged -v myvol:/var/lib/containers  quay.io/podman/stable podman run --rm -ti hello-world
Resolved short name "hello-world" to a recorded short-name alias (origin: /etc/containers/registries.conf.d/shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/hello-world:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 0e03bdcc26d7 done  
Copying config bf756fb1ae done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures

Hello from Docker!
This message shows that your installation appears to be working correctly.

To generate this message, Docker took the following steps:
 1. The Docker client contacted the Docker daemon.
 2. The Docker daemon pulled the "hello-world" image from the Docker Hub.
    (amd64)
 3. The Docker daemon created a new container from that image which runs the
    executable that produces the output you are currently reading.
 4. The Docker daemon streamed that output to the Docker client, which sent it
    to your terminal.

To try something more ambitious, you can run an Ubuntu container with:
 $ docker run -it ubuntu bash

Share images, automate workflows, and more with a free Docker ID:
 https://hub.docker.com/

For more examples and ideas, visit:
 https://docs.docker.com/get-started/
```

I also got the "docker in podman" scenario working right now:

~~~
podman run --security-opt label=disable --rm -ti  -v $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/podman/podman.sock:/var/run/docker.sock:z docker.io/library/docker run -ti --rm hello-world
~~~

Note the `--security-opt label=disable` flag on the `podman` command.

As I have no idea how to get `podman` into a UBI image (`dnf install podman` does not work!) I guess I will go with `docker` inside the image. But that is a different story.

Regarding the error message "_Error: mount /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay, flags: 0x1000: operation not permitted_":

A few months ago I also tried to run Podman in Podman. I didn't get the same error message but maybe the advice I got in
https://discussion.fedoraproject.org/t/error-executable-file-ls-not-found-in-path-no-such-file-or-directory-oci-runtime-command-not-found-error/25034/2?u=eriksjolund
 is applicable here too?

 "_The solution is to avoid the overlay on overlay situation_"

The error message I had was "_Error: executable file ls not found in $PATH: No such file or directory: OCI runtime command not found error?_"

@ctron:

> I guess I will go with docker inside the image. But that is a different story.

If you _really_ want to run this:

`$ docker run --rm -ti  -v /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock  docker run --rm -ti hello-world`

with Podman, you can do that:

```console
$ sudo systemctl start podman.socket
$ sudo podman-remote run --rm -ti -v /run/podman/podman.sock:/run/podman/podman.sock localhost/fedora-podman podman-remote run --rm -ti hello-world
Completed short name "hello-world" with unqualified-search registries (origin: /etc/containers/registries.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/hello-world:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:0e03bdcc26d7a9a57ef3b6f1bf1a210cff6239bff7c8cac72435984032851689
Copying config sha256:bf756fb1ae65adf866bd8c456593cd24beb6a0a061dedf42b26a993176745f6b
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures

Hello from Docker!
```



----

With "fedora-podman" being: (this Containerfile)
```Dockerfile
FROM fedora
RUN dnf install -y podman-remote && dnf clean all
```
EDIT: Forgot to clean first. Scary stuff. (+221M ?)
```
REPOSITORY                     TAG          IMAGE ID      CREATED         SIZE
docker.io/library/hello-world  latest       bf756fb1ae65  13 months ago   20 kB
localhost/fedora-podman        latest       a336fdc841ee  2 minutes ago   231 MB
localhost/fedora-podman        previous     2c9c53608d78  10 minutes ago  452 MB
docker.io/library/fedora       latest       a78267678b7e  4 days ago      182 MB
```

Or mix it up:
```console
$ systemctl start --user podman.socket
$ podman-remote run --rm -ti -v $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/podman/podman.sock:/run/podman/podman.sock localhost/fedora-podman podman-remote run --rm -ti hello-world
WARN[0000] failed to resize TTY: <nil>                  
Completed short name "hello-world" with unqualified-search registries (origin: /etc/containers/registries.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/hello-world:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:0e03bdcc26d7a9a57ef3b6f1bf1a210cff6239bff7c8cac72435984032851689
Copying config sha256:bf756fb1ae65adf866bd8c456593cd24beb6a0a061dedf42b26a993176745f6b
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures

Hello from Docker!
```

BTW: Is there _really_ no "Hello, World!" in the Podman registry (quay.io) ? Seems weird with the "Hello from Docker!" there...

```
Trying to pull quay.io/hello-world:latest...
  StatusCode: 404, <!DOCTYPE HTML PUBLIC "-//W3C//DTD HTML 3.2 Final/...
Error: Error initializing source docker://quay.io/hello-world:latest: Error reading manifest latest in quay.io/hello-world: StatusCode: 404, <!DOCTYPE HTML PUBLIC "-//W3C//DTD HTML 3.2 Final/...
```

At least _almost_ as weird as using the Docker socket. Maybe `podman run quay.io/fedora/fedora:rawhide echo Hello World`

We really need to write the definitive blog on this.  Running podman within podman.

I think this issue is handled

> We really need to write the definitive blog on this. Running podman within podman.

@rhatdan has the mentioned blog post been created?
Internally only, since we are waiting for the release of podman 3.2, which has new features to make it work better.
thanks for the update, looking forward to the new release =)
